mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical decline when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during supply-constrained regimes.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 4.0% triggers aggressive monetary tightening that increases borrowing costs and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 60-day equity returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10), or 20-day returns are negative in > 55% of joint expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth falls below 0.5% while inflation remains above 3.0%, supply bottlenecks erode profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the joint threshold condition fails to predict return direction with a Rank IC above 0.02.
